results.possibleIdentity: Carina Ari (1897–1970) was a Swedish dancer and choreographer who is historically linked to the founding of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.). Ari was a star of the Royal Opera in Stockholm and a main artist in the Ballets Suédois troupe in Paris, where her work in experimental performance art and film (including the choreography for the 1920 film Erotikon) established a conceptual foundation for later collaborations between artists and engineers. E.A.T. co-founder Billy Klüver was influenced by this Swedish experimental culture, and historical accounts of the movement often cite Ari’s work alongside the organization's development. Her active period as a primary influence and artist spanned the early to mid-20th century, concluding with her death in 1970.

Carina Sachs is identified in historical records as an artist or collaborator associated with Robert Rauschenberg, the co-founder of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.). She appears in Rauschenberg’s personal papers and studio correspondence, with records of contact dating to 1976. Rauschenberg's studio was a central hub for E.A.T. activities and collaborations between the mid-1960s and early 1970s, and Sachs is listed among the artists and assistants whose work and communications were archived as part of his extensive collaborative projects.
